KEYSTONE CENTER

Overview

Address
44 KEYSTONE DRIVE, LEOMINSTER, MA 01453
Phone
9785379327
Certified beds
106
Avg daily residents
69 (65% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1989-05-01
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.36 4.64 2.79 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.44 0.81 0.36 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.79 1.03 0.66 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.13 2.80 1.77 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.23 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.01 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.08
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.36
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.56
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
51.3%
annual
RN turnover
71.4%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.64
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.19
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-11-19 7 5 2 32 1 32
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-09-12 25 14 11 156 1 156
Total weighted health score
63.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (45)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

42 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2025-11-19 2025-12-03
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-11-19 2025-12-03
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2025-11-19 2025-12-03
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2025-11-19 2025-12-03
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-11-19 2025-12-03
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2025-07-29 2025-08-06
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2025-07-29 2025-08-06
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. E 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. E 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0742 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with mental disorder or psychosocial adjustment difficulty, or who has a history of trauma and/or post-traumatic stress disorder. D 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-09-12 2024-10-11
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2024-08-29 2024-09-26
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2024-08-29 2024-09-26
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-08-29 2024-09-26
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-08-29 2024-09-26
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2024-08-29 2024-09-26
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2024-07-02 2024-07-26
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2024-07-02 2024-07-26
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-02-14 2024-03-11
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-02-14 2024-03-11
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. E 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0742 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with mental disorder or psychosocial adjustment difficulty, or who has a history of trauma and/or post-traumatic stress disorder. D 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0868 Have the Quality Assessment and Assurance group have the required members and meet at least quarterly D 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0909 Regularly inspect all bed frames, mattresses, and bed rails (if any) for safety; and all bed rails and mattresses must attach safely to the bed frame. F 2023-06-27 2023-07-18
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. G 2023-05-23 2023-06-09
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2023-05-23 2023-06-09

Deficiencies 0700 — description Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. 0700.description
Deficiencies 0700 — scope_severity D 0700.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0700 — survey_date 2024-08-29 0700.survey_date
Deficiencies 0700 — tag 0700 0700.tag
Deficiencies 0726 — correction_date 2024-10-11 0726.correction_date
Deficiencies 0726 — description Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. 0726.description
Deficiencies 0726 — scope_severity E 0726.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0726 — survey_date 2024-09-12 0726.survey_date
Deficiencies 0726 — tag 0726 0726.tag
Deficiencies 0727 — correction_date 2024-10-11 0727.correction_date
Deficiencies 0727 — description Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. 0727.description
Deficiencies 0727 — scope_severity E 0727.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0727 — survey_date 2024-09-12 0727.survey_date
Deficiencies 0727 — tag 0727 0727.tag
Deficiencies 0742 — correction_date 2024-10-11 0742.correction_date
Deficiencies 0742 — correction_date 2023-07-18 0742.correction_date
Deficiencies 0742 — description Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with mental disorder or psychosocial adjustment difficulty, or who has a history of trauma and/or post-traumatic stress disorder. 0742.description
Deficiencies 0742 — description Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with mental disorder or psychosocial adjustment difficulty, or who has a history of trauma and/or post-traumatic stress disorder. 0742.description

Deficiencies 0742 — scope_severity D 0742.scope_severity

Deficiencies 0742 — tag 0742 0742.tag
Deficiencies 0756 — correction_date 2024-10-11 0756.correction_date
Deficiencies 0756 — description Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. 0756.description
Deficiencies 0756 — scope_severity D 0756.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0756 — survey_date 2024-09-12 0756.survey_date
Deficiencies 0756 — tag 0756 0756.tag
Deficiencies 0758 — correction_date 2024-10-11 0758.correction_date
Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
Deficiencies 0758 — scope_severity D 0758.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0758 — survey_date 2024-09-12 0758.survey_date
Deficiencies 0758 — tag 0758 0758.tag
Deficiencies 0760 — correction_date 2023-07-18 0760.correction_date
Deficiencies 0760 — description Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. 0760.description
Deficiencies 0760 — scope_severity D 0760.scope_severity
Deficiencies 0760 — survey_date 2023-06-27 0760.survey_date
Deficiencies 0760 — tag 0760 0760.tag
Deficiencies 0761 — correction_date 2025-12-03 0761.correction_date
Deficiencies 0761 — description Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. 0761.description
Deficiencies 0761 — scope_severity E 0761.scope_severity

Deficiencies 0842 — description Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. 0842.description
